summary.what: Document the ack-for-rebase pattern: new RebaseRelinkGuidance protocol section (prime + MCP), sharpened pending/doctor ack hints, and a DX-guide subsection
summary.why: ack already satisfies the rebase-relink requirement (structured record that counts as documented), so the cheapest correct fix was documenting the existing path rather than building content-matching
summary.how: Added RebaseRelinkGuidance const in internal/protocol, wired into both prime_workflow.go and mcp/helpers.go compose sites, replaced generic ack '...' reasons with a copy-pasteable 'rebased; content in <entry-id>' form, locked the section with a protocol test

notes: Evaluated a 5-proposal feature request. Key correction: timbers has NO content-matching today — docSet is literal SHAs — so the requester's preferred 'auto content-match relink' (A) would be built from scratch, and its naive byte-identical-diff/reflog form is unsound (rebases shift context lines; reflog is absent on fresh clones/CI). The sound version needs patch-id stored at log time = schema change. Inverted the requester's preference order to D->B->maybe-A (Gall's Law): ship the doc now, graduate to typed 'ack --to <entry>' only if the free-text link bites, build patch-id gating only with real volume evidence. Distinct from the existing stale-anchor section because there the anchor is GC'd and self-heals; here it stays reachable and does not.
